title: diplazium pseudoarboreum
intro: Diplazium pseudoarboreum, also known as the False Tree Fern, is a type of fern that belongs to the family Athyriaceae and is native to tropical and subtropical regions. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The plant is used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations of Diplazium pseudoarboreum include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are used to create teas, tonics, and capsules for medicinal purposes.
